enonce.texte: Match the diseases with their correct symptoms or descriptions.

questions.texte: Match 'malaria' with its correct description.
questions.reponse: A serious disease caused by a parasite transmitted by mosquitoes, characterized by fever, chills, and headache.

questions.texte: Match 'COVID-19' with its correct description.
questions.reponse: A respiratory disease caused by a coronavirus, with symptoms including fever, cough, and loss of taste or smell.

questions.texte: Match 'HIV/AIDS' with its correct description.
questions.reponse: A virus that attacks the immune system and can lead to acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS).

questions.texte: Match 'Ebola' with its correct description.
questions.reponse: A severe and often fatal viral disease characterized by fever, bleeding, and organ failure.

questions.texte: Match 'fever' with its correct description.
questions.reponse: An abnormally high body temperature, often a sign of infection.

questions.texte: Match 'headache' with its correct description.
questions.reponse: A continuous pain in the head, which can be a symptom of many illnesses.

questions.texte: Match 'cough' with its correct description.
questions.reponse: A reflex action to clear the airways of mucus or foreign particles.

enonce.texte:
Read the text carefully and answer the questions.

Text: Health Challenges in Cameroon

Cameroon faces significant health challenges. Malaria remains the leading cause of death, especially among children under five. The disease is transmitted by mosquitoes and is preventable through the use of bed nets and insect repellent. However, many rural areas lack access to these preventive measures. HIV/AIDS is another major health issue affecting the population. Despite progress in treatment and awareness, stigma and discrimination prevent many people from seeking testing and care. In recent years, outbreaks of cholera and other waterborne diseases have also occurred, particularly during the rainy season. The government has made efforts to improve healthcare, but the system is underfunded and understaffed. Many people in remote areas have limited access to healthcare facilities. Non-governmental organizations are working to provide health education and services to communities. The fight against these diseases requires both prevention and treatment strategies.
images: A healthcare worker in a rural clinic in Cameroon attending to patients.

questions.texte: Identify the main diseases mentioned in the text that affect Cameroon.
questions.reponse: The main diseases mentioned are malaria, HIV/AIDS, and cholera.

questions.texte: Explain why malaria is a leading cause of death in Cameroon.
questions.reponse: Malaria is transmitted by mosquitoes and many rural areas lack access to preventive measures like bed nets and insect repellent. Children under five are particularly vulnerable.

questions.texte: Analyze the reasons why many people do not seek testing and care for HIV/AIDS.
questions.reponse: Stigma and discrimination prevent many people from seeking testing and care. They fear being judged or excluded by their communities.

questions.texte: What challenges does the healthcare system in Cameroon face according to the text?
questions.reponse: The healthcare system is underfunded and understaffed. Many people in remote areas have limited access to healthcare facilities.

questions.texte: Evaluate the role of non-governmental organizations in fighting diseases in Cameroon.
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Les élèves doivent expliquer que les ONG jouent un rôle important en fournissant des services de santé et des programmes d'éducation aux communautés, surtout dans les zones où le gouvernement n'a pas les moyens d'agir.

intitule: Vocabulary in Context: Diseases and Symptoms

enonce.texte:
Complete the sentences with the correct vocabulary words from the box.

Words: malaria, COVID-19, HIV/AIDS, Ebola, fever, headache, cough, infection, virus, symptoms

questions.texte: ________ is spread by mosquitoes and causes high fever and chills.
The common ________ of flu include fever, cough, and body aches.
________ is a disease that attacks the immune system and can lead to AIDS.
The ________ spread quickly and affected many countries in West Africa.
A ________ is often the first sign that something is wrong with your health.
I have a terrible ________. I think I need to take some medicine.
She has a persistent ________ that doesn't seem to go away.
The ________ spreads through respiratory droplets when people cough or sneeze.
The wound became ________, so she had to go to the clinic.
________ is a respiratory illness that caused a global pandemic.

intitule: Grammar: Using 'should' and 'must' for Health Advice

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct modal verbs: should, shouldn't, must, mustn't.

questions.texte: You ________ wash your hands regularly to prevent infections.
People ________ drink clean water to avoid cholera.
If you have a fever, you ________ see a doctor immediately.
You ________ take antibiotics without a prescription.
Everyone ________ get vaccinated against preventable diseases.
Pregnant women ________ avoid contact with people who have infectious diseases.
You ________ ignore symptoms of malaria. It can be dangerous.
Patients with HIV ________ take their medication regularly.
People ________ cover their mouth when they cough to prevent spreading diseases.
Healthcare workers ________ wear protective equipment when treating patients.

intitule: Application: Creating a Health Awareness Campaign

enonce.texte: You are a health educator in your community. Your task is to create an awareness campaign about a disease that affects your area.
images: A poster about malaria prevention showing mosquito nets and insect repellent.

questions.texte: Choose a disease that is common in your community. Identify the causes, symptoms, and ways to prevent it.
questions.reponse: Individuelle Antwort. Les élèves peuvent choisir le paludisme, le choléra, etc., et décrire les causes (moustiques, eau contaminée), les symptômes (fièvre, diarrhée) et les mesures de prévention (moustiquaires, eau potable).

questions.texte: Design a poster or flyer to inform people about the disease. Include key information and preventive measures.
questions.reponse: Individuelle réponse. Les élèves doivent concevoir un message de sensibilisation avec des informations claires et des mesures de prévention.

questions.texte: Explain why education is important in preventing the spread of diseases.
questions.reponse: Individuelle réponse. Les élèves doivent expliquer que l'éducation permet aux gens de connaître les risques, d'adopter des comportements préventifs et de consulter un médecin en cas de symptômes.

questions.texte: Propose three actions the community can take to reduce the spread of the disease you chose.
questions.reponse: Individuelle réponse. Exemples: 1. Organiser des campagnes de nettoyage pour éliminer les eaux stagnantes. 2. Distribuer des moustiquaires aux familles. 3. Sensibiliser sur l'importance de l'eau potable.

intitule: Case Study: Managing a Disease Outbreak

enonce.texte:
Read the case study and answer the questions.

Case Study: Cholera Outbreak in a Village

In a small village in Cameroon, there has been a sudden increase in cases of cholera. Cholera is a waterborne disease that causes severe diarrhea and dehydration. The outbreak is linked to contaminated water from a nearby river. Many villagers drink this water because they have no access to clean water. The village health center is overwhelmed with patients. There are not enough medical supplies or staff to treat everyone. The government has sent a team of health workers to help, but the situation remains critical.
images: A cholera treatment center with patients and healthcare workers.

questions.texte: Identify the cause of the cholera outbreak in the village.
questions.reponse: The outbreak is caused by contaminated water from a nearby river.

questions.texte: Explain why cholera spreads quickly in the village.
questions.reponse: Cholera spreads quickly because villagers have no access to clean water and they drink water from the contaminated river.

questions.texte: Analyze the challenges faced by the village health center.
questions.reponse: The health center is overwhelmed with patients. There are not enough medical supplies or staff to treat everyone. The situation is critical.

questions.texte: What solutions would you propose to stop the outbreak and prevent future cases? Justify your answers.
questions.reponse: Individuelle réponse. Les élèves doivent proposer des solutions comme la distribution d'eau potable, la construction de puits, la sensibilisation à l'hygiène, et le renforcement des capacités des centres de santé.

enonce.texte: Complete the table by forming the correct word from the base word provided.

questions.texte: prevent (verb) → ________ (noun)
infect (verb) → ________ (noun)
treat (verb) → ________ (noun)
transmit (verb) → ________ (noun)
symptom (noun) → ________ (adjective)
prevent (verb) → ________ (adjective)
infectious (adjective) → ________ (noun)
health (noun) → ________ (adjective)

intitule: Listening and Comprehension: A Public Health Announcement

enonce.texte:
Read the public health announcement and answer the questions.

Public Health Announcement: Preventing COVID-19

The Ministry of Health reminds all citizens to take precautions against COVID-19. The virus spreads through respiratory droplets when people cough, sneeze, or speak. To protect yourself and others, you should: wash your hands frequently with soap and water, wear a mask in crowded places, maintain social distancing, and avoid large gatherings. If you experience symptoms such as fever, cough, or difficulty breathing, you should get tested immediately. It is important to stay informed and follow official guidelines. Together, we can stop the spread.
images: A poster with COVID-19 prevention tips: washing hands, wearing a mask, and social distancing.

questions.texte: How does COVID-19 spread according to the announcement?
questions.reponse: COVID-19 spreads through respiratory droplets when people cough, sneeze, or speak.

questions.texte: List three preventive measures mentioned in the announcement.
questions.reponse: 1. Wash hands frequently with soap and water. 2. Wear a mask in crowded places. 3. Maintain social distancing.

questions.texte: What should someone do if they experience symptoms of COVID-19?
Write a summary (about 50 words) of the announcement in your own words.
questions.reponse: Individuelle réponse. Les élèves doivent résumer les informations clés: comment le virus se propage, les mesures de prévention et l'importance de se faire tester en cas de symptômes.
